name: DMV Accountants
bestFit: Business owners who need cleaner books, tax planning, or funding preparation support.
referralRelevance: Accountants can spot cash-flow strain and documentation gaps early.
fundingRelatedNote: Clean financials can support a stronger funding conversation, though approval is never guaranteed.

name: DMV Bookkeepers
bestFit: Owners who need better transaction cleanup, reporting, and cash-flow visibility.
referralRelevance: Bookkeepers often see cash-flow gaps before owners admit there is a problem.
fundingRelatedNote: Better records can help owners understand what to prepare before applying.

name: DMV Business Attorneys
bestFit: Owners dealing with contracts, entity issues, leases, disputes, or growth complexity.
referralRelevance: Attorneys can identify funding needs tied to contracts, disputes, expansion, or restructuring.
fundingRelatedNote: Legal clarity can matter when funding is connected to contracts, ownership, or collateral.

name: DMV Chamber and Networking Groups
bestFit: Local owners looking for business community, introductions, events, and education.
referralRelevance: Community leaders frequently know which owners need capital before the market does.
fundingRelatedNote: Events and workshops can route owners toward readiness tools before they apply.

name: Veteran Business Resources
bestFit: Veteran-owned businesses looking for practical funding readiness and local ecosystem support.
referralRelevance: Veteran resource groups can connect operators with tools, guidance, and partner paths.
fundingRelatedNote: Veteran-owned firms still need realistic preparation and no-guarantee funding guidance.

name: Commercial Realtors
bestFit: Businesses considering expansion, second locations, buildouts, or lease changes.
referralRelevance: Commercial realtors see capital needs around buildouts, moves, and expansion timing.
fundingRelatedNote: Funding conversations should happen before a lease commitment turns into a cash-flow trap.
